notes:
**Fixes & polish** — this release closes twenty-nine data-safety and correctness
bugs found by the new editor conformance program (see *Under the hood*). The
themes, plainly:

- **Your writing can no longer be lost to timing.** Saves from two places at
  once — the main editor, a backlink card, a zoomed view, another device via
  sync — now merge row by row instead of overwriting each other; a save that's
  still writing is always waited for before the app quits or backgrounds; and a
  note that fails to load shows a safe read-only state instead of an empty
  editor whose save could wipe the real content. (PRD Addendum 16; Addendum 20
  §T1: zoom is "a view, never a data change.")
- **Undo is safe right after opening a note.** Undo can no longer revert past
  what was loaded from disk and blank the note; restoring a version from
  History now saves your latest edits first, and the restore itself is a single
  clean undo step.
- **Zoom keeps everything.** Content arriving from sync — or an image you drop —
  while you're zoomed into a block now survives zooming out, on every exit
  path; an edit-free zoom round-trip no longer rewrites the note.
- **Titles and renames stick.** A title you typed but never clicked away from
  now survives quitting, backgrounding, and tab switches; renaming a note no
  longer discards the last keystrokes typed in one of its backlink cards.
- **Turning a line into a heading or bullet keeps its identity.** The `# ` and
  `- ` shortcuts no longer break `((block references))` pointing at that block
  or orphan its indented children. (PRD Addendum 2 §B; blocks are the
  referenceable unit.)
- **Checkboxes and panel edits always count.** Ticking a todo in a note you
  hadn't clicked into first is no longer silently dropped.
- **Precise interactions.** The keyboard context-menu key acts on the block
  your caret is in (never the top of the document); forward-Delete before an
  image or chip deletes it in exactly one press; collapsing a section moves
  your caret out of the hidden area so keystrokes never vanish; a drag
  interrupted by the system cleanly cancels; pasting from Windows apps no
  longer leaks clipboard header text into the note; the drag grip no longer
  disappears while aiming at nested bullets.
- **International text is safe.** Emoji families, flags, and skin tones are
  never split by truncation or caret movement, and invalid characters can no
  longer be persisted.

**Under the hood**

- A cross-platform **editor conformance suite** (`docs/testing/editor-conformance/`):
  1,406 behavioral scenarios with stable ids across 21 dimensions — the
  bring-up contract for every Infiniti editor host. Over 600 now enforced in
  CI across unit, harness, and real-engine (Chromium + WebKit) tiers; it found
  every bug this release fixes.
- CI now runs the full e2e suite on every develop push, uploads failure traces,
  and isolates parallel dev servers per checkout.
